summary.intent: Refactor the gossamer desktop app (a Claude Code session viewer) into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction, with the core indexing logic extracted into a reusable package.
summary.outcome: Monorepo structure established with packages/core (extracted indexing/DB logic) and packages/vscode (extension scaffold with sessions screen); extension reaches a partially working state but debugging of server startup is still in progress.

repo: Entire.io writes Claude Code hook events to a dedicated git branch in the repo; gossamer reads that branch and indexes it into SQLite via Prisma/better-sqlite3
Entire.io's presence in a repo is detected via .entire/settings.json at the repo root
The gossamer server entrypoint is dist/serve.js; the root-level build must be run before the VS Code extension can spawn it
Prisma client is generated into packages/core/prisma/generated; path references from src/ files must use ../prisma/... not ../../prisma/...

code.finding: Prisma client import path must be ../prisma/generated/client relative to src/
Must use system 'node' binary (resolved via PATH) not process.execPath, which points to VS Code's bundled Electron Node — causes NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch with better-sqlite3
Vite builds ES modules; script tag must include type='module' or the browser silently ignores it, causing blank webview
root path must be absolute (using __dirname) not relative, otherwise vite build fails with UNRESOLVED_ENTRY when invoked from repo root via npm -w
Set rollupOptions.output.entryFileNames/chunkFileNames/assetFileNames to deterministic names (no hash) so GossamerPanel.ts can reference assets/index.js and assets/index.css predictably

workflow: VS Code extension development logs go to Debug Console (not Output panel) when launched via F5

When a VS Code extension imports native Node modules (better-sqlite3 via Prisma), the extension host runs VS Code's bundled Node version which may differ from the system version — spawning a child process with system node sidesteps this entirely
The gossamer server does a Prisma schema push on startup which takes several seconds; the webview must wait for a ready signal before fetching or it gets connection errors
npm workspace -w flag requires the workspace's 'name' field from package.json, not the directory name

friction: better-sqlite3 native module compiled against different NODE_MODULE_VERSION than VS Code's bundled Node (141 vs 140) — required architectural pivot to spawn server as child process
Vite hashes output filenames by default, breaking hardcoded asset references in GossamerPanel.ts
Vite build fails with UNRESOLVED_ENTRY when root is a relative path and build is invoked from repo root
VS Code extension logs not visible in Output panel — only in Debug Console, causing confusion during debugging
Server startup race condition: webview fetches sessions before the server finishes schema push, resulting in 'error loading sessions'
process.execPath in VS Code extension host resolves to Electron's node, not system node — not obvious without debugging
open_items: Server startup debugging incomplete — extension still not confirmed working end-to-end as of session end
Electron app (app/) left in broken state intentionally; needs cleanup or deprecation later
packages/core CLI entrypoint (gossamer-index) deferred — only module API exposed for now
SessionDetail screen and other screens beyond ActiveSessions not yet ported to VS Code extension
better-sqlite3 rebuild story for extension packaging/publishing not resolved (npm rebuild needed for distribution)
UI redesign deferred — existing Mantine components reused as-is pending later reevaluation
Daemon/background capture mode for terminal-only Claude Code usage explicitly deferred to post-v1
